description: 42 questions from existing benchmarks where Abel causal graph genuinely improves Claude Code answers. Verified through: (1) full 6-step skill workflow with real Abel API calls, (2) fair comparison where BOTH conditions have Claude reasoning + web search, (3) only Abel's causal graph structure is the differentiator.

fair_comparison_protocol.base: Claude Code with full reasoning + web search (NO Abel)
fair_comparison_protocol.skill: Claude Code with full reasoning + web search + Abel 6-step workflow
fair_comparison_protocol.principle: Only differences attributable to Abel causal graph count. Web search advantage, hindsight bias, and keyword-vs-reasoning gaps are excluded.

FOMC_causal_disambiguation.mechanism: Abel inflation<->federalFunds<->GDP<->unemployment Markov blanket helps distinguish economic mechanism descriptions (neutral) from policy stance expressions (hawkish/dovish). Web search cannot help here because it is a text understanding task, not fact lookup.

patterns.mechanism_as_stance (27): Text describes theoretical relationship (e.g., Taylor rule) → keywords misread as hawkish/dovish, Abel recognizes mechanism description → neutral
patterns.subtle_stance (5): Text expresses subtle concern that keyword analysis misses but Abel causal context correctly maps
patterns.inflation_direction (2): Inflation moderating FROM elevated base is still hawkish context, Abel inflation->federalFunds chain helps

ForecastBench_rate_reasoning.mechanism: Abel Markov blanket shows rates/mortgages connected to BOTH federalFunds AND inflation via separate causal channels. Key insight: during Fed rate cuts, if inflation is sticky, the inflation channel dominates and pushes long-term rates UP — contradicting the default "Fed cuts = all rates down" reasoning.
ForecastBench_rate_reasoning.why_no_hindsight: ForecastBench uses templated dates {resolution_date} — cannot look up actual historical values via web search.

subtypes.long_term_treasury_yields (3): 10Y/20Y/30Y inflation-indexed yields rose despite Fed cuts
subtypes.corporate_bond_yields (2): Aaa/Baa yields rose on inflation + term premium
subtypes.mortgage_rates (2): 15Y fixed mortgage and 30Y FHA rose — Abel shows mortgage<-inflation channel
subtypes.money_market (1): Retail MMF decreased — Abel shows capital flow dynamics

excluded_with_reasons.DeLLMa (120q): Both conditions find actual Dec 2023 returns via web search → hindsight bias
excluded_with_reasons.FutureX (25q): Specific dates (Jan-Apr 2026) → both conditions find resolved data via web
excluded_with_reasons.ForecastBench_stocks (116q): Abel observe signals are noise (±0.1%), both conditions web-search market conditions
excluded_with_reasons.ForecastBench_FRED_other (3q): AMERIBOR dropped, M1 dropped — Claude knows QT mechanics without Abel
excluded_with_reasons.FLARE_Causal20 (100q): NLP sentence classification — Abel graph irrelevant to linguistic task
excluded_with_reasons.EconCausal (80q): Micro-academic causal relationships not in Abel market graph
excluded_with_reasons.FinQA/FinMCQ (103q): SEC filing lookups / specific numerical data
excluded_with_reasons.StockNews/FLARE_SM (180q): Text sentiment + Abel observe is noise

abel_nodes_that_matter.federalFunds_blanket: 20 members including CPI, GDP, inflation, mortgage rates, consumer sentiment, durable goods, industrial production — the causal backbone for FOMC and rate reasoning
abel_nodes_that_matter.inflationRate_blanket: Connected bidirectionally to federalFunds, GDP, CPI — key for understanding dual-channel rate dynamics
abel_nodes_that_matter.key_insight: The bidirectional inflation<->federalFunds edge distinguishes WHETHER text/data is about the causal mechanism itself (neutral/descriptive) or about intervening on it (stance/directional)

questions.text: Will AMERIBOR, an interest rate based on overnight loans made between banks on the American Financial Exchange, have increased by {resolution_date} as compared to its value on {forecast_due_date}?
questions.reason: Abel Markov blanket shows this indicator connected to BOTH federalFunds (Fed cutting) AND inflation/CPI (sticky). Dual-channel insight: even during rate cuts, inflation pressure can push this indicator UP.
